title: Surfaces, Draw Order, Alpha, Transforms, Assets, and Presentation
description: Every frame reloads assets, repeatedly rotates transformed images, mixes alpha modes, draws UI behind the world, converts before display setup, and calls flip from several objects. Learners cumulatively produce owned layered rendering and asset pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish display and off-screen surfaces, pixel formats, per-surface and per-pixel alpha, color keys, locks, and conversion timing.
Define clear, background, world, effects, HUD, focus, diagnostics, and presentation order once per frame.
Rotate and scale from an immutable source, recenter output bounds, cache justified variants, and avoid cumulative degradation.
